2:T405,{"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"FAQPage","mainEntity":[{"@type":"Question","name":"How many days a week should a beginner workout?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Beginners should start with 3 days per week, focusing on full-body workouts. This allows adequate recovery while building a fitness habit. After 4-6 weeks, you can increase to 4 days using an upper/lower split."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What exercises should beginners do?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Beginners should focus on compound exercises that work multiple muscle groups: squats, deadlifts, bench press, rows, overhead press, and pull-ups/lat pulldowns. These give the best results for time invested."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How much weight should beginners lift?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Start with a weight you can lift for 2-3 more reps than your target. For example, if your goal is 10 reps, use a weight you could lift 12-13 times. Focus on perfect form before increasing weight."}}]}7:{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}

Exercises, sets, reps, rest periods, and a proven 4-week progression plan."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-2xl p-6 md:p-8 mb-12","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"flex items-start gap-4","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"p-3 bg-orange-500/10 rounded-xl","children":["$","$L3",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M17.596 12.768a2 2 0 1 0 2.829-2.829l-1.768-1.767a2 2 0 0 0 2.828-2.829l-2.828-2.828a2 2 0 0 0-2.829 2.828l-1.767-1.768a2 2 0 1 0-2.829 2.829z","key":"9m4mmf"}],["path",{"d":"m2.5 21.5 1.4-1.4","key":"17g3f0"}],["path",{"d":"m20.1 3.9 1.4-1.4","key":"1qn309"}],["path",{"d":"M5.343 21.485a2 2 0 1 0 2.829-2.828l1.767 1.768a2 2 0 1 0 2.829-2.829l-6.364-6.364a2 2 0 1 0-2.829 2.829l1.768 1.767a2 2 0 0 0-2.828 2.829z","key":"1t2c92"}],["path",{"d":"m9.6 14.4 4.8-4.8","key":"6umqxw"}]],"className":"lucide-dumbbell w-6 h-6 text-orange-400"}]}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"text-lg font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"Get a Personalized Beginner Plan"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400 mb-4","children":"Our AI creates a custom workout based on your experience, equipment, and goals."}],["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/workout-generator","className":"inline-flex items-center gap-2 px-6 py-3 bg-gradient-to-r from-orange-500 to-orange-600 text-white rounded-full font-medium hover:from-orange-600 hover:to-orange-700 transition-all","children":["Generate My Plan",["$","$L3",null,{"iconNode":[["path",{"d":"M5 12h14","key":"1ays0h"}],["path",{"d":"m12 5 7 7-7 7","key":"xquz4c"}]],"className":"lucide-arrow-right w-4 h-4"}]]}]]}]]}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose prose-lg max-w-none prose-headings:text-white prose-p:text-slate-400 prose-strong:text-white prose-li:text-slate-400","children":[["$","h2",null,{"children":"Before You Start"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Starting a workout routine can feel overwhelming. There are countless exercises, conflicting advice, and complex programs. ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"This guide cuts through the noise."}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["As a beginner, your goal is simple: ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"build a habit"}]," while learning proper form on fundamental movements. Don't worry about advanced techniques or perfect optimization yet."]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"The Beginner Workout Philosophy"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Consistency beats intensity:"}]," 3 moderate workouts per week beats 1 brutal session"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Compound movements first:"}]," Exercises that work multiple muscles give the best return on time"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Progressive overload:"}]," Gradually increase weight or reps each week"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Recovery is training:"}]," Muscles grow during rest, not during workouts"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"The 4-Week Beginner Workout Plan"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["This plan uses a ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"full-body approach"}]," 3 days per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday). Each workout hits all major muscle groups."]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Week 1: Learning Form"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/40 rounded-xl p-6 not-prose my-6","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-orange-400 font-medium mb-4","children":"Workout A (Mon/Fri)"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-2 text-slate-400 text-sm","children":[["$","p",null,{"children":"1. Goblet Squat: 3 sets × 10 reps (2 min rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"2. Push-ups (or Knee Push-ups): 3 sets × 8-12 reps (90s rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"3. Dumbbell Row: 3 sets × 10 reps/side (90s rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"4. Glute Bridge: 3 sets × 12 reps (60s rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"5. Plank: 3 sets × 20-30 seconds (60s rest)"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-orange-400 font-medium mb-4 mt-6","children":"Workout B (Wed)"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-2 text-slate-400 text-sm","children":[["$","p",null,{"children":"1. Romanian Deadlift: 3 sets × 10 reps (2 min rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"2. Dumbbell Bench Press: 3 sets × 10 reps (90s rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"3. Lat Pulldown (or Resistance Band Pull): 3 sets × 10 reps (90s rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"4. Walking Lunges: 3 sets × 10 reps/leg (90s rest)"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"5. Dead Bug: 3 sets × 8 reps/side (60s rest)"}]]}]]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Week 2: Adding Volume"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Same exercises, add 1 set to each movement:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"All exercises: 4 sets instead of 3"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Keep reps the same"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Focus on controlled tempo (2 seconds down, 1 second up)"}]]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Week 3: Increasing Weight"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Increase weight by the smallest increment possible (usually 2.5-5 lbs):"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"If you completed all reps last week with good form, increase weight"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"If you couldn't complete all reps, keep the same weight"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Never sacrifice form for heavier weight"}]]}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Week 4: Testing Progress"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Try to increase weight on 2-3 exercises. Note how you feel:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":"Are movements feeling more natural?"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Is your recovery improving?"}],["$","li",null,{"children":"Do you feel stronger?"}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Exercise Instructions"}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Goblet Squat"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Hold a dumbbell vertically at chest level. Feet shoulder-width apart. Squat down until thighs are parallel to floor. Keep chest up and knees tracking over toes."}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Dumbbell Row"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Place one knee and hand on a bench. Hold dumbbell in other hand. Pull weight to hip, squeezing shoulder blade. Lower with control."}],["$","h3",null,{"children":"Romanian Deadlift"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Hold dumbbells in front of thighs. Hinge at hips, pushing butt back while keeping legs nearly straight. Lower until you feel hamstring stretch. Squeeze glutes to stand."}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Progression After 4 Weeks"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"After completing this plan, you have several options:"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Repeat with heavier weights:"}]," Continue the same structure but increase loads"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Move to 4-day upper/lower split:"}]," More volume for faster progress"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Add more exercises:"}]," Include lateral raises, curls, and extensions"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Use our AI Workout Generator:"}]," Get a personalized plan based on your progress"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Common Beginner Mistakes"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Skipping warm-up:"}]," 5 minutes of light cardio prevents injury"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Lifting too heavy too soon:"}]," Master form before adding weight"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Inconsistent schedule:"}]," Pick days you can stick to"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Neglecting nutrition:"}]," Protein intake matters for recovery"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","strong",null,{"children":"Comparing to others:"}]," Your only competition is yesterday's you"]}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"children":"Related Resources"}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/blog/beginner-gym-plan","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Beginner Gym Plan"}]," — Alternative 4-week gym-based program"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/workout-generator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"AI Workout Generator"}]," — Personalized plans after this routine"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/one-rep-max","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"1RM Calculator"}]," — Track strength gains"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":[["$","$L4",null,{"href":"/tools/protein-calculator","className":"text-orange-400 hover:text-orange-300","children":"Protein Calculator"}]," — Optimize recovery nutrition"]}]]}]]}],["$","section",null,{"className":"mt-16","children":[["$","h2",null,{"className":"text-2xl font-bold text-white mb-8","children":"Frequently Asked Questions"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"space-y-6","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"How many days a week should a beginner workout?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Beginners should start with 3 days per week, focusing on full-body workouts. This allows adequate recovery while building a fitness habit."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"What exercises should beginners do?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Focus on compound exercises: squats, deadlifts, bench press, rows, overhead press, and pull-ups. These work multiple muscle groups and give the best results."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-slate-900/60 backdrop-blur-sm border border-slate-800 rounded-xl p-6","children":[["$","h3",null,{"className":"font-bold text-white mb-2","children":"How much weight should beginners lift?"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-slate-400","children":"Start with a weight you can lift for 2-3 more reps than your target.
